title: phegopteris subsimilis
intro: Phegopteris subsimilis, also known as the False Maidenhair fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris subsimilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
